levels.title: Dots & Variables
levels.dialogue_pre: LEAH (DM): Welcome to the dotbook. Today you write a dot into variables, then you *call the move*. Syntax: name = number. Then: band.step_to("DM", target_x, target_y, counts=8).
levels.hint_text: JACOB (PERC): counts is your metronome. If you set counts = 8, you just wrote an 8-count phrase.
levels.dialogue_post: LEAH (DM): Clean. One dot, one arrival. That's how we start getting memorable.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 1 • LESSON 1 (DOTS + VARIABLES + YOUR FIRST CALL)
#
# Think like a drum major: you don't guess. You write the dot, then you call it.
#
# Python pattern:
#   variable_name = number
#
# Band move pattern:
#   band.step_to(name, x, y, counts=8)
#
# Your target dot today is (20, 11).

# 1) Write the dot into variables
target_x = 0  # TODO: 20
target_y = 0  # TODO: 11

# 2) Set your phrase length
counts = 0  # TODO: 8

# 3) Call the move using your variables
band.step_to("DM", target_x, target_y, counts=counts)

levels.title: Two Dots, One Phrase
levels.dialogue_pre: LEAH (DM): Two dots today. But here's the difference: *you* write the calls. Dot A then Dot B. Two phrases. Clean and readable.
levels.hint_text: LEAH (DM): Example call: band.step_to("DM", 20, 11, counts=8). Write one for A, then one for B.
levels.dialogue_post: LEAH (DM): Nice. You just wrote a mini drill chart: A then B.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 1 • LESSON 2 (TWO DOTS, TWO CALLS)
#
# Goal: move DM to Dot A, then Dot B.
# Dot A = (20, 11)
# Dot B = (28, 11)
#
# You are writing the calls today. Copy the pattern, change the variables:
#   band.step_to("DM", dotA_x, dotA_y, counts=8)

# 1) Store the dots
dotA_x = 0  # TODO: 20
dotA_y = 0  # TODO: 11
dotB_x = 0  # TODO: 28
dotB_y = 0  # TODO: 11

# 2) TODO: Call Dot A (8 counts)

# 3) TODO: Call Dot B (8 counts)

levels.title: Counts Control
levels.dialogue_pre: JACOB (PERC): Counts are the click. You're going to write two moves again, but this time you control the tempo: 4 counts (fast) then 8 counts (steady).
levels.hint_text: JACOB (PERC): Set fast_counts = 4 and slow_counts = 8. Then write two step_to calls using counts=fast_counts and counts=slow_counts.
levels.dialogue_post: JACOB (PERC): That's tempo discipline. You told the band exactly how long to take.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 1 • LESSON 3 (COUNTS CONTROL)
#
# Goal: Same dots, different FEEL.
# Dot A = (20, 11) in 4 counts
# Dot B = (28, 11) in 8 counts

dotA_x, dotA_y = 20, 11
dotB_x, dotB_y = 28, 11

# 1) Set your tempos
fast_counts = 0  # TODO: 4
slow_counts = 0  # TODO: 8

# 2) TODO: March Dot A using counts=fast_counts

# 3) TODO: March Dot B using counts=slow_counts

levels.title: Phrase + Rest
levels.dialogue_pre: LEAH (DM): Now we rehearse like a real ensemble: phrase, breathe, phrase. You will write: step_to A, wait 8, step_to B.
levels.hint_text: JACOB (PERC): band.wait(counts=8) is marking time. Put it BETWEEN your two step_to calls.
levels.dialogue_post: LEAH (DM): Clean transitions. The space between moves is part of the show.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 1 • LESSON 4 (PHRASE + REST + PHRASE)
#
# Goal: Move to Dot A (8), WAIT (8), then Dot B (8).
# Dot A = (20, 11)
# Dot B = (28, 11)

dotA_x, dotA_y = 20, 11
dotB_x, dotB_y = 28, 11

# 1) TODO: Step to Dot A (8 counts)

# 2) TODO: Mark time for 8 counts

# 3) TODO: Step to Dot B (8 counts)

levels.title: Week 1 Test: Copper Ridge
levels.dialogue_pre: LEAH (DM): Competition rep. You already rehearsed the pattern. Now prove it: Dot A at count 8, Dot B at count 16. No extra moves.
levels.hint_text:
VOSS (OBSIDIAN): "How quaint. We call that the *Intro-to-Excellence* set."
VOSS (OBSIDIAN): "Try not to trip over your own semicolons."
JACOB (PERC): He's performing for the judges. You perform for the click. Two phrases. counts=8 both times.
levels.dialogue_post: LEAH (DM): That's a clean rep. Let him be smug. We're building something real.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 1 • TEST (COMPETITION)
#
# Copper Ridge is watching.
# Requirements:
#   1) Hit Dot A at count 8:  (20, 11)
#   2) Hit Dot B at count 16: (28, 11)
#   3) Total counts must be EXACTLY 16 (two 8-count phrases).
#
# This is the same pattern you ran all week:
#   step_to A (8)
#   step_to B (8)

dotA_x, dotA_y = 20, 11
dotB_x, dotB_y = 28, 11

# TODO 1) Hit Dot A

# TODO 2) Hit Dot B

levels.title: Set the Interval
levels.dialogue_pre: ANNA (HIGH WW): Clean lines come from clean patterns. Use a loop to place a section with even spacing.
levels.hint_text: ANNA (HIGH WW): Use spacing=2. Then x = x0 + i*spacing inside the loop.
levels.dialogue_post: ANNA (HIGH WW): Nice. When your pattern is right, the whole form reads like music.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 2: LOOPS + INTERVALS
# Goal: Spawn W1..W5 on y=11 starting at x=14, spacing=2.
# Fix the spacing, then run.

y = 11
x0 = 14
spacing = 0  # TODO: set this to 2

for i in range(5):
    name = f"W{i+1}"
    band.spawn(name, section="WINDS", x=x0 + i*spacing, y=y)

levels.title: Lock the Tempo
levels.dialogue_pre: JACOB (PERC): We move on the click, not on vibes. Set your counts like a metronome.
levels.hint_text: DOMINIC (LOW BRASS): Use wait() to hold the pocket. Then move the next phrase on time.
levels.dialogue_post: JACOB (PERC): That was a clean split. No rushing, no dragging.
levels.starter_code:
band.step_to('W1', 16, 13, counts=4)
band.step_to('W2', 16, 15, counts=4)
# Percussion holds
band.wait(4)
# Swap: winds hold, percussion moves
band.step_to('P1', 16, 7, counts=4)
band.step_to('P2', 16, 9, counts=4)

levels.title: Dynamic Decision
levels.dialogue_pre: KYLE (HIGH BRASS): Power is useless if we collide. Make the smart call and keep the form clean.
levels.hint_text: KYLE (HIGH BRASS): If the lane is crowded, take more counts or go around.
levels.dialogue_post: KYLE (HIGH BRASS): That is leadership: adapt without panicking.
levels.starter_code:
tx, ty = 16, 11
# If the direct path is blocked, go around
if band.is_occupied(10, 11):
    band.step_to('B1', 10, 9, counts=4)
    band.step_to('B1', tx, ty, counts=4)
else:
    band.step_to('B1', tx, ty, counts=8)

levels.title: Visual Phrase
levels.dialogue_pre: ANNA (HIGH WW): A show has phrases. In code, phrases are functions you can reuse.
levels.hint_text: MYLEE (LOW WW): Make the arc helper once, then call it. Smooth transitions, no panic.
levels.dialogue_post: ANNA (HIGH WW): That felt musical. Now we can build bigger moments from small, clean ideas.
levels.starter_code:
# WEEK 5: FUNCTIONS (PHRASES)
#
# Imports are blocked in the game sandbox.
# Use band.math for trig (sin/cos/radians).


# Make an arc helper built from step_to()
def arc_out(names, cx, cy, radius, start_deg, end_deg, counts):
    for i, name in enumerate(names):
        t = i / max(1, (len(names)-1))
        a = band.math.radians(start_deg + (end_deg-start_deg)*t)
        x = int(round(cx + band.math.cos(a)*radius))
        y = int(round(cy + band.math.sin(a)*radius))
        band.step_to(name, x, y, counts=counts)

arc_out(
    ['G1','G2','G3'],
    cx=14, cy=11,
    radius=4,
    start_deg=200, end_deg=340,
    counts=8,
)
